subject: [CLAMP] AAF certificate using certinializer
commit_message:
[CLAMP] AAF certificate using certinializer

use of auto-generated certificates via AAF side-car
at OOM deployment time for CLAMP.

Issue-ID: CLAMP-884

Change-Id: I5392059fc997f3432109da8a15e86517842a77aa
Signed-off-by: osgn422w <gervais-martial.ngueko@intl.att.com>

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Please use certInitializer template instead of this one. This one is to be obsoleted shortly (after we migrate last users)

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> Please use certInitializer template instead of this one. This one is to be obsoleted shortly (after we migrate last users)

Which template precisely, where can I find that template?

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/5260/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/5260

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > Please use certInitializer template instead of this one. This one is to be obsoleted shortly (after we migrate last users)
> 
> Which template precisely, where can I find that template?

The one that you shouldn't use are located in common/common/templates/_aafconfig.tpl

The one that you should use is called certInitializer and is located in common/certInitializer

If you are looking for some example how to use it just checkout for example NBI or jest grep for certInitializer outside of common dir

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(13 comments)

Hello,
thanks for the merge request.
Main issue is kubernetes/clamp/charts/clamp-backend/templates/deployment.yaml#L50 (and kubernetes/clamp/templates/deployment.yaml#L49) which would prevent clamp to start as expected.

kubernetes/clamp/resources/config/default.conf#L5 may have also some impacts.
Rest is more editorial 😊

Please note also that the commit message (on OOM) must follow this form:

[NAME_OF_COMPONENT|DOC|COMMON|GENERIC] Meaningful title (from OOM side)

at least one sentence explaining the change done in this patch, cause and consequences and possibly more of course

Issue-ID: AS_WE_ARE_FORCED_BUT_MEANINGLESS
Change-ID: xxx
Sign-off: xxx

Commit message will be the last stuff that will stay with our code so it must clearly explain the changes, the "why" and the consequences. If it change OOM behavior in any way, documentation must be also updated.

Starting Frankfurt branching, merge requests which are not following this pattern will not be merged.

Please read the following pages and follow the guidelines for writing commit message contained therein.

    http://bit.ly/goodcommitmessages
    http://who-t.blogspot.com/2009/12/on-commit-messages.html
    http://dep.debian.net/deps/dep3/
